title: xDeck Package Manifest
description: Manifest contract for tenants/_packages/<name>/package.json. Locked under ADR 0002 (2026-04-29). Additive over the pre-2026-04-29 schema; existing 5 packages stay valid without modification.

namespace.description: Builder-owned namespace prefix. Required. Slice A1 enforces against the static allowlist at docs/config/package-namespaces.json; ROADMAP §9.42 replaces that with builder_profile lookups.

name.description: Lowercase kebab-case identifier within the namespace. Qualified id is `<namespace>/<name>`.

kind.description: Install-behaviour discriminator. Existing packages omit this and default to feature. §9.52 — `entitlement` ships NO classes (no per-tenant code execution); the entire feature is centralised behind §9.50 capabilities + a billing block. Adding `entryClass` / `triggers` to an entitlement manifest is rejected at content-validation time.

system.description: §9.74 — when true, the package is auto-installed at tenant provisioning (`install_system_packages` step), hidden from tenant marketplace listing, and uninstall is restricted to super_admin (`Force uninstall` with reason). The bar is high — every system package is forced on every tenant forever. Existing packages omit this and stay 100% backward-compatible (defaults false).

reversible.description: IRREVERTABLE packages (when false): can be installed ONCE on a tenant but can NEVER be uninstalled — `uninstall` hard-fails with PACKAGE_IRREVERSIBLE regardless of role/force. For system-of-record packages whose tables hold data that must persist (identity, audit/compliance, financial ledgers, vertical entity packs once they carry customer data) — uninstalling would be silent data loss. The only way to remove the data is to deprovision the whole tenant. Defaults true (normal install/uninstall). A `false` opt-in should be paired with a loud install-time confirmation. CI tests an irrevertable package via provision→install→deprovision, never install→uninstall.

category.description: Free-form (e.g. Communication, Reporting, AI, Insights, Localization, Branding, Vertical:Healthcare). Marketplace UI groups by this string.

entryClass.description: Optional. Class loaded by the runtime for this package's primary use case.

entities.description: Legacy flat entity list (still accepted). Prefer provides.entities + requires.entities.

extensible.description: Slice A2 — when true, the migrator adds a `customFields jsonb DEFAULT '{}'` column so tenants can ship sidecar extensions under tenants/<slug>/entities/extensions/<ns>__<name>.json.

inheritable.description: Slice A3 — when true, tenants may declare entities that inherit from this one via Joined Table Inheritance (child table whose primary key FKs the parent's, ON DELETE CASCADE).

sti.description: Slice B5 — opts the entity into Single-Table Inheritance. Tenants declare STI children that share this entity's table, distinguished by the discriminator column.

oneOf.description: Legacy bare-name form. Each must have index.tsx + meta.json under components/<Name>/.

Slot-mounted contribution. The host shell iterates installed packages and mounts every component whose `slot` matches a registered shell slot (e.g. `root-providers`). Used by `standard/telemetry` to bootstrap the browser SDK without tenant repos bundling it directly.

slot.description: Host-shell slot id this component mounts at. Reserved slots: `root-providers` (top of React tree, before routing); host shell rejects unknown slots at load time.

file.description: Path to the component source, relative to the package root (e.g. `ui/TelemetryProvider.tsx`).

components.description: Component contributions. Bare strings are the legacy `components/<Name>/` lookup; object entries declare slot-mounted contributions resolved by the host shell.

tokens.description: Path to design tokens JSON. Required when kind == theme.

surfaces.description: Surface ID -> override descriptor map. Required when kind == theme.

config.description: Default config values. Tenants override via packages[].config in tenant config.json.

configSchema.description: Optional JSON Schema for the package's config field. Validator checks tenant config[].config against this at install time.

settingsSchema.description: §9.80 — typed, owner-editable post-install settings + secrets descriptor. The platform renders a uniform form from `fields` (§9.81 <SchemaForm>), validates submitted values, persists non-secret values per-tenant in tenant_package_setting.values, and routes `secret` fields into the per-tenant encrypted vault. Distinct from `configSchema` (JSON-Schema for the runtime `config` block). PackageValidatorService.checkSettingsSchema runs the deeper per-field shape check via validateConfigSchema; AJV here validates the outer shape.

capabilities.description: §9.50 — runtime-API capability declarations. `required` must be granted at install time; `optional` are individually toggle-able. Each entry must be a known capability per the @xdeck/runtime-api catalogue (e.g. 'email:send', 'entity:read:Patient'). Reserved strings ('event:emit', 'event:listen', 'class:invoke:*') are rejected per §9.50.9.1. Content validation (catalogue + reserved) runs in PackageValidatorService.validate(); AJV here validates shape only.

provides.description: Runtime-API capabilities this package EMITS — exposes via the §9.50 `xdeck.*` global to other installed packages running in the same tenant. The host's capability bridge enforces grants per consumer; declaring a capability here is the supply side. Used by `standard/telemetry` so any installed package can call `xdeck.telemetry.track(...)` without bundling the SDK directly.

tenant_docs_contributions.description: §9.54 — declarative tenant-doc updates applied to the tenant's _ai/*.md files on install (insert_or_update removed on uninstall; append is write-once). Sanctioned paths, per-path operation rules, and the 500-char cap are enforced by TenantDocsContributionValidator; AJV here validates shape only.

telemetry.description: §9.50 RA3 — declared telemetry events this package emits. Tenant admins can audit the catalogue before install (DPDP / GDPR readiness); ingest-side validation (RA5) consumes the same declarations to reject malformed payloads. Builders who emit events should declare them here; future codegen targets emit `.d.ts` types from this block so `xdeck.telemetry.track()` is type-checked against the per-package catalogue.

events.description: Map of event name → declaration. Event names are namespaced by the package itself — declaring `appointment_booked` here from `acme/booking-flow` produces the qualified id `acme/booking-flow#appointment_booked` in the catalogue, preventing cross-package name collisions.

schema.description: JSON Schema (draft-07-compatible) for the event payload. AJV-validated at publish + ingest time. Authors should declare every field the SDK will receive; unknown fields are rejected by the server-side scrubber.

description.description: One-line explanation of when this event fires + what it represents. Shown in the tenant admin event-catalog UI.

retentionDays.description: Override the package-level retention for this specific event. Useful when a regulatory boundary requires (e.g.) a 7-year retention on `prescription_dispensed` even when the rest of the package retains 30 days.

category.description: Privacy / consent category. Maps to the tenant's `standard/telemetry` `config.consent.categories` allowlist so a tenant can opt out of `marketing` events without disabling `essential` ones.

events.description: Keys must match the event-name pattern `^[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$`.
